economic_notes: Nation has investments in Katzie Development Corporation Ltd., Katzie Master GP Ltd., and Katzie Master Limited Partnership accounted for using modified equity method. During 2023, the Nation became a Limited Partner in Katzie Master Limited Partnership and transferred its limited partnership interest in Katzie Development Limited Partnership to Katzie Master Limited Partnership at fair market value. Earnings from Nation business entities were $2,599,265.
notes: Fiscal year ends March 31, 2023. Six entities fully consolidated including Katzie First Nation Bus Corporation, Smuq'wa Development Corporation, Katzie Cultural Education Society, Katzie Early Years Society, Katzie First Nation Housing Society, and Katzie First Nation Affordable Housing Development Society. Three GBEs accounted for under modified equity method: Katzie Development Corporation Ltd., Katzie Master GP Ltd., and Katzie Master Limited Partnership. New accounting standards adopted April 1, 2022: PS 3450 Financial Instruments and PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations. Remeasurement gains of $1,616,450 recorded separately (portfolio investments and foreign exchange). Accumulated surplus includes $1,616,450 in accumulated remeasurement gains. Total_government_transfers calculated as ISC $5,632,356 + Province of BC $2,180,035 + First Nations Health Authority $1,078,730 + Government of Canada $555,055 + CMHC $150,587 + Fisheries and Oceans Canada $1,295,000 + B.C. Treaty Commission $645,000 + Surrey Schools $150,407 + Sto:lo Aboriginal Skills & Employment Training $88,551 + First Nations Education Steering Committee $27,766 + New Relationship Trust $40,000 + Fraser Health Authority $5,000 - Repayment of funding $72,099 = $11,776,388. Health and Social Programs segment presented as combined; split not available. No schedule of remuneration included in the document provided.
